title: China Economy - South China Morning Post
description: China economy news, including budget, the stock market and analysis.

items.title: China accelerates digital yuan push as 26 banks join new cross-border platform
items.creator: Alice Li
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China accelerates digital yuan push as 26 banks join new cross-border platform
items.content:
China has onboarded a first batch of 26 domestic and overseas financial institutions to its integrated cross-border digital yuan payment platform, marking a further step in building out the digital currency’s payment infrastructure amid Beijing’s push to scale up adoption of the digital yuan, also known as the e-CNY.
The institutions will join as direct participants in China’s Cross-border e-CNY Transfer Services (CBETS), which is managed by the People’s Bank of China.
The CBETS supports...

items.title: Global brands in China seek local partners amid market squeeze
items.creator: Neil Denslow,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Global brands in China seek local partners amid market squeeze
items.content:
Pizza Hut, the largest casual dining operator in China, has become the latest Western chain to change hands in the country, as global brands count on local companies to bolster sales in an increasingly competitive market.
Yum China will pay US$1.2 billion to buy the China business from US-based Yum! Brands, according to a statement on Tuesday. The Shanghai-based company already operates the pizza chain in China, along with KFC, Taco Bell and hotpot brands. The all-cash sale is due to close in...

items.title: China vows new legal shield that could counter US sanctions and protect finance
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China vows new legal shield that could counter US sanctions and protect finance
items.content:
China has vowed to strengthen legal protections against unilateral sanctions, according to Vice-Premier He Lifeng, as Beijing moves to build a robust legal shield for its financial system and companies facing rising geopolitical risks.
Addressing the Lujiazui Forum in Shanghai on Wednesday, He said China would incorporate blocking and countermeasure provisions into upcoming financial legislation and expand legal tools to counter what it considers to be improper extraterritorial sanctions.
“We...

items.title: Shanghai to lead ‘financial superpower’ push as China unveils plan to boost yuan use
items.creator: Frank Chen,Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Shanghai to lead ‘financial superpower’ push as China unveils plan to boost yuan use
items.content:
Top Chinese financial regulators have vowed to accelerate Shanghai’s transformation into a full-fledged global financial hub and encourage the yuan’s overseas adoption, as Beijing seeks to tout China’s stability amid volatility in the global market.
Speaking in Shanghai on Wednesday at an annual financial gathering known as the Lujiazui Forum, policymakers such as People’s Bank of China governor Pan Gongsheng also said that the city would continue to serve as a vital conduit for offshore finance...

items.title: Europe is gearing up for a trade fight with China. But will it change anything?

items.translated_title: Europe is gearing up for a trade fight with China. But will it change anything?
items.content:
As trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels continue to rise, China’s firms in the European Union have been forced to walk a delicate tightrope: expanding their presence in the lucrative market while grappling with heightened regulatory hurdles and rapid geopolitical shifts. In the second part of this three-part series, we look at whether China and the EU are heading for a full-blown trade conflict.
Last week, Beijing gathered more than a dozen Chinese companies in Berlin for a forum with a...

items.title: High-speed and visa-free: how China is cruising to become the world’s top tourism market
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: High-speed and visa-free: how China is cruising to become the world’s top tourism market

images.alt: Foreign tourists visit Qilou Old Street in Haikou, in China’s southern island province of Hainan on May 9. Photo: Xinhua
images.title: Foreign tourists visit Qilou Old Street in Haikou, in China’s southern island province of Hainan on May 9. Photo: Xinhua

items.content:
An expansion of visa-free entry, the buildout of transportation networks and growing travel interest within China’s borders – including among budget-conscious citizens – are positioning the country to become the world’s top tourism market, analysts said.
China’s travel sector, valued at US$1.8 trillion last year, could reach US$3.5 trillion by 2036, the World Travel and Tourism Council (WTTC) trade association said in its 2026 Economic Impact Research report released on June 3. The United...

items.title: China’s green energy drive shores up factories against El Nino, limiting outage odds
items.creator: He Huifeng
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s green energy drive shores up factories against El Nino, limiting outage odds
items.content:
In recent years, when extreme summer heat has seen electricity demand peak, manufacturers in southern China’s Guangdong province have asked the same question: will power shortages return?
In 2021 and 2022, China’s small and medium-sized manufacturers were hit by power shortages that triggered production curbs and factory shutdowns, disrupting supply chains in sectors ranging from electronics to metals. Tight coal supply and surging overseas orders all contributed to the shortages.
This summer,...

items.title: Motorsport new battleground for Chinese carmakers as Geely and BYD rev up global drive

items.translated_title: Motorsport new battleground for Chinese carmakers as Geely and BYD rev up global drive
items.content:
Billionaire Li Shufu, owner and chairman of Geely Auto, China’s second-largest carmaker, plans to bring the Superbike World Championship (WSBK) to the country, banking on the success of a home-grown motorcycle brand in competition.
The move marked Geely’s latest step to sharpen its image as an international marque with advanced technologies and new models, according to analysts.
Li and Zhang Xue, owner of ZXMOTO, agreed to jointly promote China’s hosting of the WSBK during their appearance on...

items.title: China to raise power prices for energy-intensive sectors in green transition push
items.creator: Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China to raise power prices for energy-intensive sectors in green transition push
items.content:
Beijing plans to increase electricity prices on nine energy-intensive sectors in a move to speed up the green transition and drive a new wave of industrial upgrading.
The National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) and four other ministries announced a three-year road map on Monday that pledged to promote energy-saving measures and cut carbon emissions across heavy industry, including the iron, steel, oil refining and aluminium sectors.
The most direct measure is a power price increase...

items.title: China’s retail sales fall for first time since Covid
items.creator: Neil Denslow,Raymond Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s retail sales fall for first time since Covid
items.content:
China’s retail sales shrank for the first time in more than three years as higher fuel prices and inflation caused by the war on Iran compounded the nation’s consumption challenges.
May sales fell by 0.6 per cent from a year earlier, missing the 0.1 per cent growth forecast among economists polled by Chinese financial data provider Wind. It was the first decline since Covid lockdowns ended in December 2022. Other government data releases on Tuesday showed a 4.1 per cent drop in fixed-asset...

items.title: China sees first retail sales drop in over 3 years, prompting talk of stimulus
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat,Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China sees first retail sales drop in over 3 years, prompting talk of stimulus
items.content:
China reported sluggish retail sales figures in May even as exports skyrocketed, highlighting persistent weakness in domestic demand and leading analysts to predict the deployment of additional stimulus measures to address the widening imbalance.
Retail sales, a key gauge of consumer spending, fell by 0.6 per cent year on year in May, the first decline since China lifted its Covid-19 pandemic lockdown at the end of 2022 and down from a 0.2 per cent increase in April, according to data released...

items.title: Can Hong Kong break London’s maritime insurance grip?
items.creator: Chow Chung-yan
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Can Hong Kong break London’s maritime insurance grip?
items.content:
The once-mighty Royal Navy is now but a shadow of its former self, plagued by years of budget cuts and management scandals. It has shrunk to its smallest size since the Napoleonic Wars. Lord Nelson must be turning in his grave.
Yet despite the sorry state of its fleet, Britain remains the lord of the seven seas, albeit in a different fashion. Together, the Anglo-Saxons on either side of the Atlantic control the oceans and global shipping without needing to deploy a single vessel – a phenomenon...

items.content:
As trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels continue to rise, China’s firms in the European Union have been forced to walk a delicate tightrope: expanding their presence in the lucrative market while grappling with heightened regulatory hurdles and rapid geopolitical shifts. In the first part of this three-part series, we look at a new, complex EU carbon tariff system that has business owners scratching their heads.
Neil Miao has been exporting metal hardware to Europe for years. But earlier...

items.title: After US-Iran accord and oil-price plunge, analysts warn of ‘grey area’ for Chinese firms

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: After US-Iran accord and oil-price plunge, analysts warn of ‘grey area’ for Chinese firms
items.content:
As plunging oil prices signal broad market optimism following an initial deal between the United States and Iran, attention has turned to whether Chinese firms sanctioned for Iran-related business will see immediate relief, though analysts remain cautious.
“New purchases of Iranian oil ostensibly wouldn’t be subject to fresh sanctions, but at the same time, I imagine we won’t see a rush to lift existing sanctions on Chinese importers of Iranian oil,” said Lynn Song, chief economist for Greater...

items.title: Will a US-Iran deal ease pressure on China’s sanctions-hit firms?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Xinyi Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Will a US-Iran deal ease pressure on China’s sanctions-hit firms?

images.alt: Tugboats move a crude oil tanker to its berth in Qingdao, in China’s eastern Shandong province. The US has sanctioned several Chinese independent, or “teapot”, refineries that purchase Iranian crude. Photo: AFP
images.title: Tugboats move a crude oil tanker to its berth in Qingdao, in China’s eastern Shandong province. The US has sanctioned several Chinese independent, or “teapot”, refineries that purchase Iranian crude. Photo: AFP

items.content:
A tentative US-Iran deal has eased immediate fears of escalation, but the fine print around sanctions relief – following months of aggressive US enforcement under the “Economic Fury” campaign – remains highly contested and legally intricate.
The South China Morning Post unpacks the key sanctions-related terms in the agreement, the intense US sanctions campaign against Chinese entities since the outbreak of the Iran conflict in late February, and what a potential waiver or termination of those...

items.title: China credit data shows sluggish demand, more household deleveraging amid weak investment

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China credit data shows sluggish demand, more household deleveraging amid weak investment
items.content:
Beijing’s long-standing efforts to reinvigorate the domestic economy continue to face headwinds, as the latest credit data points to persistently sluggish consumption and weak investment across China, with the tech sector standing out as a lone bright spot amid the central government’s push for innovation to reduce external reliance.
New bank loans totalled 520 billion yuan (US$77 billion) in May, according to People’s Bank of China data released on Friday. The figure trailed the 620 billion...

items.title: China’s first C919s get critical ‘C-checks’ as European regulators watch
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s first C919s get critical ‘C-checks’ as European regulators watch

images.alt: China’s C919 has entered an important phase, with jets across the series undergoing rigorous inspections. Photo: Handout
images.title: China’s C919 has entered an important phase, with jets across the series undergoing rigorous inspections. Photo: Handout

items.content:
Three years after its maiden commercial flight as China’s flagship civilian airliner, the C919 is entering a pivotal maintenance phase, with jets across the series being progressively grounded for rigorous inspections.
The results of these safety-focused “C-checks” are expected to play a role in convincing European aviation regulators of the aircraft’s long-term airworthiness.
State-owned carrier China Southern Airlines finished the first C-check of its C919 fleet at the end of May. The process...

items.title: More Chinese graduates explore gig work as pay growth trails national averages: report
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: More Chinese graduates explore gig work as pay growth trails national averages: report
items.content:
China’s growing army of university graduates is opting for flexible work and pursuing higher degrees less frequently, while growth in starting salaries lags national averages, according to a Beijing-based education consultancy.
Over the past five years, the share of university graduates seeking flexible jobs rose 2.7 percentage points to 6.9 per cent, from 4.2 per cent in 2021, with a slightly higher bump among those from vocational colleges, the MyCOS Research Institute found in its annual...

items.title: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food safety issues’ (原标题: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food-safety issues’)

items.translated_title: China summons executives of Walmart-owned Sam’s Club over ‘food safety issues’
items.content:
Chinese authorities have summoned representatives of US-based membership-only retailer Sam’s Club for an “accountability interview” in response to “frequent food-safety issues”, the State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) announced on Monday.
According to a notice from the regulator, the Walmart-owned supermarket chain was urged to “prioritise food safety, strictly fulfil social corporate responsibilities, mitigate risks across the entire supply chain and safeguard public health” in...
